root.name: cache
tip: U: cache dir
U: cache info
U: cache list [<pattern>] [--format=[human, abspath]]
U: cache remove <pattern>
U: cache purge
Inspect and manage pip's wheel cache.

option.name: --format
tip: U: --format <list_format>
Select the output format among: human (default) or abspath.

next.name: human
tip: Default value

next.name: dir
tip: Show the cache directory.

next.name: info
tip: Show information about the cache.

next.name: list
tip: U: list [<pattern>] [--format=[human, abspath]]
List filenames of packages stored in the cache.
<pattern> can be a glob expression or a package name.

next.name: purge
tip: Remove all items from the cache.

next.name: remove
tip: U: remove <pattern>
Remove one or more package from the cache.

root.name: check
tip: U: check [options]
Verify installed packages have compatible dependencies.

root.name: completion
tip: A helper command used for command completion.

option.name: --bash
tip: U: -b, --bash
Emit completion code for bash.

option.name: --fish
tip: U: -f, --fish
Emit completion code for fish.

option.name: --powershell
tip: U: -p, --powershell
Emit completion code for powershell.

option.name: --zsh
tip: U: -z, --zsh
Emit completion code for zsh.

root.name: config
tip: U: config [<file-option>] list
U: config [<file-option>] [--editor <editor-path>] edit
U: config [<file-option>] get command.option
U: config [<file-option>] set command.option value
U: config [<file-option>] unset command.option
U: config [<file-option>] debug
Manage local and global configuration.

option.name: --editor
tip: U: --editor <editor>
Editor to use to edit the file.
Uses VISUAL or EDITOR environment variables if not provided.

option.name: --global
tip: Use the system-wide configuration file only.

option.name: --site
tip: Use the current environment configuration file only.

option.name: --user
tip: Use the user configuration file only.

next.name: debug
tip: List the configuration files and values defined under them.

next.name: edit
tip: Edit the configuration file in an editor.

next.name: get
tip: U: get command.option
Get the value associated with command.option.

next.name: list
tip: List the active configuration (or from the file specified).

next.name: set
tip: U: set command.option value
Set the command.option=value.

next.name: unset
tip: U: unset command.option
Unset the value associated with command.option.

root.name: debug
tip: U: debug <options>
Display debug information.

option.name: --abi
tip: U: --abi <abi>
Only use wheels compatible with Python abi <abi>, e.g. 'pypy_41'.
If not specified, then the current interpreter abi tag is used.
Use this option multiple times to specify multiple abis supported by the target interpreter.
Generally you will need to specify --implementation, --platform, and --python-version when using this option.

option.name: --implementation
tip: U: --implementation <implementation>
Only use wheels compatible with Python implementation <implementation>, e.g. 'pp', 'jy', 'cp', or 'ip'.
If not specified, then the current interpreter implementation is used.
Use 'py' to force implementation-agnostic wheels.

option.name: --platform
tip: U: --platform <platform>
Only use wheels compatible with <platform>.
Defaults to the platform of the running system.
Use this option multiple times to specify multiple platforms supported by the target interpreter.

option.name: --python-version
tip: U: --python-version <python_version>
The Python interpreter version to use for wheel and 'Requires-Python' compatibility checks.
Defaults to a version derived from the running interpreter.

root.name: download
tip: U: download [options] <requirement specifier> [package-index-options] ...
U: download [options] -r <requirements file> [package-index-options] ...
U: download [options] <vcs project url> ...
U: download [options] <local project path> ...
U: download [options] <archive url/path> ...
Download packages from PyPI, VCS project urls, local project directories, or local or remote source archives.

option.name: --abi
tip: U: --abi <abi>
Only use wheels compatible with Python abi <abi>, e.g. 'pypy_41'.
If not specified, then the current interpreter abi tag is used.
Use this option multiple times to specify multiple abis supported by the target interpreter.
Generally you will need to specify --implementation, --platform, and --python-version when using this option.

option.name: --all-releases
tip: U: --all-releases <release_control>
Allow all release types (including pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to allow pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --build-constraint
tip: U: --build-constraint <file>
Constrain build dependencies using the given constraints file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --check-build-dependencies
tip: Check the build dependencies.

option.name: --constraint
tip: U: -c, --constraint <file>
Constrain versions using the given constraints file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --dest
tip: U: -d, --dest <dir>
Download packages into <dir>.

option.name: --extra-index-url
tip: U: --extra-index-url <url>
Extra URLs of package indexes to use in addition to --index-url.
Should follow the same rules as --index-url.

option.name: --find-links
tip: U: -f, --find-links <url>
If a URL or path to an html file, then parse for links to archives such as sdist (.tar.gz) or wheel (.whl) files.
If a local path or file:// URL that's a directory, then look for archives in the directory listing.
Links to VCS project URLs are not supported.

option.name: --group
tip: U: --group <[path:]group>
Install a named dependency-group from a 'pyproject.toml' file.
If a path is given, the name of the file must be 'pyproject.toml'.
Defaults to using 'pyproject.toml' in the current directory.

option.name: --ignore-requires-python
tip: Ignore the Requires-Python information.

option.name: --implementation
tip: U: --implementation <implementation>
Only use wheels compatible with Python implementation <implementation>, e.g. 'pp', 'jy', 'cp', or 'ip'.
If not specified, then the current interpreter implementation is used.
Use 'py' to force implementation-agnostic wheels.

option.name: --index-url
tip: U: -i, --index-url <url>
Base URL of the Python Package Index (default https://pypi.org/simple).
This should point to a repository compliant with PEP 503 (the simple repository API) or a local directory laid out in the same format.

option.name: --no-binary
tip: U: --no-binary <format_control>
Do not use binary packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all binary packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Note that some packages are tricky to compile and may fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --no-build-isolation
tip: Disable isolation when building a modern source distribution.
Build dependencies specified by PEP 518 must be already installed if this option is used.

option.name: --no-clean
tip: Don't clean up build directories.

option.name: --no-deps
tip: Don't install package dependencies.

option.name: --no-index
tip: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs instead).

option.name: --only-binary
tip: U: --only-binary <format_control>
Do not use source packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all source packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Packages without binary distributions will fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --only-final
tip: U: --only-final <release_control>
Only allow final releases (no pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --platform
tip: U: --platform <platform>
Only use wheels compatible with <platform>.
Defaults to the platform of the running system.
Use this option multiple times to specify multiple platforms supported by the target interpreter.

option.name: --pre
tip: Include pre-release and development versions.
By default, pip only finds stable versions.

option.name: --prefer-binary
tip: Prefer binary packages over source packages, even if the source packages are newer.

option.name: --progress-bar
tip: U: --progress-bar <progress_bar>
Specify whether the progress bar should be used.
In 'auto' mode, --quiet will suppress all progress bars.

next.name: auto
tip: Default value

option.name: --python-version
tip: U: --python-version <python_version>
The Python interpreter version to use for wheel and 'Requires-Python' compatibility checks.
Defaults to a version derived from the running interpreter.

option.name: --require-hashes
tip: Require a hash to check each requirement against, for repeatable installs.
This option is implied when any package in a requirements file has a --hash option.

option.name: --requirement
tip: U: -r, --requirement <file>
Install from the given requirements file.
The file or URL can be in pip's requirements.txt format, or pylock.toml format.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --requirements-from-script
tip: U: --requirements-from-script <file>
Install dependencies of the given script file as defined by PEP 723 inline metadata.

option.name: --src
tip: U: --src <dir>
Directory to check out editable projects into.
The default in a virtualenv is '<venv path>/src'.
The default for global installs is '<current dir>/src'.

option.name: --uploaded-prior-to
tip: U: --uploaded-prior-to <datetime_or_duration>
Only consider packages uploaded prior to the given value.
Accepts an ISO 8601 datetime (e.g., '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z', uses local timezone if none specified) or a duration in days (e.g., 'P3D' for packages uploaded at least 3 days ago).
Only effective when installing from indexes that provide upload-time metadata.

root.name: freeze
tip: U: freeze [options]
Output installed packages in requirements format.
Packages are listed in a case-insensitive sorted order.

option.name: --all
tip: Do not skip these packages in the output: pip

option.name: --exclude
tip: U: --exclude <package>
Exclude specified package from the output.

option.name: --exclude-editable
tip: Exclude editable package from output.

option.name: --local
tip: U: -l, --local
If in a virtualenv that has global access, do not output globally-installed packages.

option.name: --path
tip: U: --path <path>
Restrict to the specified installation path for listing packages (can be used multiple times).

option.name: --requirement
tip: U: -r, --requirement <file>
Use the order in the given requirements file and its comments when generating output.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --user
tip: Only output packages installed in user-site.

root.name: hash
tip: U: hash [options] <file> ...
Compute a hash of a local package archive.
These can be used with --hash in a requirements file to do repeatable installs.

option.name: --algorithm
tip: U: -a, --algorithm <algorithm>
The hash algorithm to use.

root.name: help
tip: U: help <command>
Show help for commands.

root.name: index
tip: U: index versions <package>
Inspect information available from package indexes.

tip: U: --abi <abi>

tip: U: --platform <platform>
Only use wheels compatible with <platform>.

root.name: inspect
tip: U: inspect [options]
Inspect the content of a Python environment and produce a report in JSON format.

option.name: --local
tip: If in a virtualenv that has global access, do not list globally-installed packages.

option.name: --path
tip: U: --path <path>
Restrict to the specified installation path for listing packages (can be used multiple times).

option.name: --user
tip: Only output packages installed in user-site.

root.name: install
tip: U: install [options] <requirement specifier> [package-index-options] ...
U: install [options] -r <requirements file> [package-index-options] ...
U: install [options] [-e] <vcs project url> ...
U: install [options] [-e] <local project path> ...
U: install [options] <archive url/path> ...
Install packages from PyPI, VCS project urls, local project directories, or local or remote source archives.

option.name: --abi
tip: U: --abi <abi>
Only use wheels compatible with Python abi <abi>, e.g. 'pypy_41'.
If not specified, then the current interpreter abi tag is used.
Use this option multiple times to specify multiple abis supported by the target interpreter.
Generally you will need to specify --implementation, --platform, and --python-version when using this option.

option.name: --all-releases
tip: U: --all-releases <release_control>
Allow all release types (including pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to allow pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --break-system-packages
tip: Allow pip to modify an EXTERNALLY-MANAGED Python installation.

option.name: --build-constraint
tip: U: --build-constraint <file>
Constrain build dependencies using the given constraints file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --check-build-dependencies
tip: Check the build dependencies.

option.name: --compile
tip: Compile Python source files to bytecode.

option.name: --config-settings
tip: U: -C, --config-settings <settings>
Configuration settings to be passed to the build backend.
Settings take the form KEY=VALUE.
Use multiple --config-settings options to pass multiple keys to the backend.

option.name: --constraint
tip: U: -c, --constraint <file>
Constrain versions using the given constraints file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --dry-run
tip: Don't actually install anything, just print what would be.
Can be used in combination with --ignore-installed to 'resolve' the requirements.

option.name: --editable
tip: U: -e, --editable <path/url>
Install a project in editable mode (i.e. setuptools 'develop mode') from a local project path or a VCS url.

option.name: --extra-index-url
tip: U: --extra-index-url <url>
Extra URLs of package indexes to use in addition to --index-url.
Should follow the same rules as --index-url.

option.name: --find-links
tip: U: -f, --find-links <url>
If a URL or path to an html file, then parse for links to archives such as sdist (.tar.gz) or wheel (.whl) files.
If a local path or file:// URL that's a directory, then look for archives in the directory listing.
Links to VCS project URLs are not supported.

option.name: --force-reinstall
tip: Reinstall all packages even if they are already up-to-date.

option.name: --group
tip: U: --group <[path:]group>
Install a named dependency-group from a 'pyproject.toml' file.
If a path is given, the name of the file must be 'pyproject.toml'.
Defaults to using 'pyproject.toml' in the current directory.

option.name: --ignore-installed
tip: U: -I, --ignore-installed
Ignore the installed packages, overwriting them.
This can break your system if the existing package is of a different version or was installed with a different package manager!

option.name: --ignore-requires-python
tip: Ignore the Requires-Python information.

option.name: --implementation
tip: U: --implementation <implementation>
Only use wheels compatible with Python implementation <implementation>, e.g. 'pp', 'jy', 'cp', or 'ip'.
If not specified, then the current interpreter implementation is used.
Use 'py' to force implementation-agnostic wheels.

option.name: --index-url
tip: U: -i, --index-url <url>
Base URL of the Python Package Index (default https://pypi.org/simple).
This should point to a repository compliant with PEP 503 (the simple repository API) or a local directory laid out in the same format.

option.name: --no-binary
tip: U: --no-binary <format_control>
Do not use binary packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all binary packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Note that some packages are tricky to compile and may fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --no-build-isolation
tip: Disable isolation when building a modern source distribution.
Build dependencies specified by PEP 518 must be already installed if this option is used.

option.name: --no-clean
tip: Don't clean up build directories.

option.name: --no-compile
tip: Do not compile Python source files to bytecode.

option.name: --no-deps
tip: Don't install package dependencies.

option.name: --no-index
tip: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs instead).

option.name: --no-warn-conflicts
tip: Do not warn about broken dependencies.

option.name: --no-warn-script-location
tip: Do not warn when installing scripts outside PATH.

option.name: --only-binary
tip: U: --only-binary <format_control>
Do not use source packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all source packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Packages without binary distributions will fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --only-final
tip: U: --only-final <release_control>
Only allow final releases (no pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --platform
tip: U: --platform <platform>
Only use wheels compatible with <platform>.
Defaults to the platform of the running system.
Use this option multiple times to specify multiple platforms supported by the target interpreter.

option.name: --pre
tip: Include pre-release and development versions.
By default, pip only finds stable versions.

option.name: --prefer-binary
tip: Prefer binary packages over source packages, even if the source packages are newer.

option.name: --prefix
tip: U: --prefix <dir>
Installation prefix where lib, bin and other top-level folders are placed.
Note that the resulting installation may contain scripts and other resources which reference the Python interpreter of pip, and not that of --prefix.
See also the --python option if the intention is to install packages into another (possibly pip-free) environment.

option.name: --progress-bar
tip: U: --progress-bar <progress_bar>
Specify whether the progress bar should be used.
In 'auto' mode, --quiet will suppress all progress bars.

next.name: auto
tip: Default value

option.name: --python-version
tip: U: --python-version <python_version>
The Python interpreter version to use for wheel and 'Requires-Python' compatibility checks.
Defaults to a version derived from the running interpreter.

option.name: --report
tip: U: --report <file>
Generate a JSON file describing what pip did to install the provided requirements.
Can be used in combination with --dry-run and --ignore-installed to 'resolve' the requirements.
When - is used as file name it writes to stdout.
When writing to stdout, please combine with the --quiet option to avoid mixing pip logging output with JSON output.

option.name: --require-hashes
tip: Require a hash to check each requirement against, for repeatable installs.
This option is implied when any package in a requirements file has a --hash option.

option.name: --requirement
tip: U: -r, --requirement <file>
Install from the given requirements file.
The file or URL can be in pip's requirements.txt format, or pylock.toml format.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --requirements-from-script
tip: U: --requirements-from-script <file>
Install dependencies of the given script file as defined by PEP 723 inline metadata.

option.name: --root
tip: U: --root <dir>
Install everything relative to this alternate root directory.

option.name: --root-user-action
tip: U: --root-user-action <root_user_action>
Action if pip is run as a root user.

next.name: warn
tip: Default value

option.name: --src
tip: U: --src <dir>
Directory to check out editable projects into.
The default in a virtualenv is '<venv path>/src'.
The default for global installs is '<current dir>/src'.

option.name: --target
tip: U: -t, --target <dir>
Install packages into <dir>.
By default this will not replace existing files/folders in <dir>.
Use --upgrade to replace existing packages in <dir> with new versions.

option.name: --upgrade
tip: U: -U, --upgrade
Upgrade all specified packages to the newest available version.
The handling of dependencies depends on the upgrade-strategy used.

option.name: --upgrade-strategy
tip: U: --upgrade-strategy <upgrade_strategy>
Determines how dependency upgrading should be handled [default: only-if-needed].
'eager'          - dependencies are upgraded regardless of whether the currently installed version satisfies the requirements of the upgraded package(s).
'only-if-needed' - are upgraded only when they do not satisfy the requirements of the upgraded package(s).

option.name: --uploaded-prior-to
tip: U: --uploaded-prior-to <datetime_or_duration>
Only consider packages uploaded prior to the given value.
Accepts an ISO 8601 datetime (e.g., '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z', uses local timezone if none specified) or a duration in days (e.g., 'P3D' for packages uploaded at least 3 days ago).
Only effective when installing from indexes that provide upload-time metadata.

option.name: --user
tip: Install to the Python user install directory for your platform.
Typically ~/.local/, or %APPDATA%\Python on Windows.
(See the Python documentation for site.USER_BASE for full details.)

root.name: list
tip: U: list [options]
List installed packages, including editables.
Packages are listed in a case-insensitive sorted order.

option.name: --all-releases
tip: U: --all-releases <release_control>
Allow all release types (including pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to allow pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --editable
tip: U: -e, --editable
List editable projects.

option.name: --exclude
tip: U: --exclude <package>
Exclude specified package from the output.

option.name: --exclude-editable
tip: Exclude editable package from output.

option.name: --extra-index-url
tip: U: --extra-index-url <url>
Extra URLs of package indexes to use in addition to --index-url.
Should follow the same rules as --index-url.

option.name: --find-links
tip: U: -f, --find-links <url>
If a URL or path to an html file, then parse for links to archives such as sdist (.tar.gz) or wheel (.whl) files.
If a local path or file:// URL that's a directory, then look for archives in the directory listing.
Links to VCS project URLs are not supported.

option.name: --format
tip: U: --format <list_format>
Select the output format among: columns (default), freeze, or json.
The 'freeze' format cannot be used with the --outdated option.

next.name: columns
tip: Default value

option.name: --include-editable
tip: Include editable package in output.

option.name: --index-url
tip: U: -i, --index-url <url>
Base URL of the Python Package Index (default https://pypi.org/simple).
This should point to a repository compliant with PEP 503 (the simple repository API) or a local directory laid out in the same format.

option.name: --local
tip: U: -l, --local
If in a virtualenv that has global access, do not list globally-installed packages.

option.name: --no-binary
tip: U: --no-binary <format_control>
Do not use binary packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all binary packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Note that some packages are tricky to compile and may fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --no-index
tip: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs instead).

option.name: --not-required
tip: List packages that are not dependencies of installed packages.

option.name: --only-binary
tip: U: --only-binary <format_control>
Do not use source packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all source packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Packages without binary distributions will fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --only-final
tip: U: --only-final <release_control>
Only allow final releases (no pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --outdated
tip: U: -o, --outdated
List outdated packages.

option.name: --path
tip: U: --path <path>
Restrict to the specified installation path for listing packages (can be used multiple times).

option.name: --pre
tip: Include pre-release and development versions.
By default, pip only finds stable versions.

option.name: --prefer-binary
tip: Prefer binary packages over source packages, even if the source packages are newer.

option.name: --uploaded-prior-to
tip: U: --uploaded-prior-to <datetime_or_duration>
Only consider packages uploaded prior to the given value.
Accepts an ISO 8601 datetime (e.g., '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z', uses local timezone if none specified) or a duration in days (e.g., 'P3D' for packages uploaded at least 3 days ago).
Only effective when installing from indexes that provide upload-time metadata.

option.name: --uptodate
tip: U: -u, --uptodate
List uptodate packages.

option.name: --user
tip: Only output packages installed in user-site.

root.name: search
tip: U: search [options] <query>
Search for PyPI packages whose name or summary contains <query>.

option.name: --index
tip: U: -i, --index <url>
Base URL of Python Package Index (default https://pypi.org/pypi).

root.name: show
tip: U: show [options] <package> ...
Show information about one or more installed packages.
The output is in RFC-compliant mail header format.

option.name: --files
tip: U: -f, --files
Show the full list of installed files for each package.

root.name: uninstall
tip: U: uninstall [options] <package> ...
U: uninstall [options] -r <requirements file> ...
Uninstall packages.

option.name: --break-system-packages
tip: Allow pip to modify an EXTERNALLY-MANAGED Python installation.

option.name: --requirement
tip: U: -r, --requirement <file>
Uninstall all the packages listed in the given requirements file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --root-user-action
tip: U: --root-user-action <root_user_action>
Action if pip is run as a root user.

next.name: warn
tip: Default value

option.name: --yes
tip: U: -y, --yes
Don't ask for confirmation of uninstall deletions.

root.name: wheel
tip: U: wheel [options] <requirement specifier> ...
U: wheel [options] -r <requirements file> ...
U: wheel [options] [-e] <vcs project url> ...
U: wheel [options] [-e] <local project path> ...
U: wheel [options] <archive url/path> ...
Build Wheel archives for your requirements and dependencies.

option.name: --all-releases
tip: U: --all-releases <release_control>
Allow all release types (including pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to allow pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --build-constraint
tip: U: --build-constraint <file>
Constrain build dependencies using the given constraints file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --check-build-dependencies
tip: Check the build dependencies.

option.name: --config-settings
tip: U: -C, --config-settings <settings>
Configuration settings to be passed to the build backend.
Settings take the form KEY=VALUE.
Use multiple --config-settings options to pass multiple keys to the backend.

option.name: --constraint
tip: U: -c, --constraint <file>
Constrain versions using the given constraints file.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --editable
tip: U: -e, --editable <path/url>
Install a project in editable mode (i.e. setuptools 'develop mode') from a local project path or a VCS url.

option.name: --extra-index-url
tip: U: --extra-index-url <url>
Extra URLs of package indexes to use in addition to --index-url.
Should follow the same rules as --index-url.

option.name: --find-links
tip: U: -f, --find-links <url>
If a URL or path to an html file, then parse for links to archives such as sdist (.tar.gz) or wheel (.whl) files.
If a local path or file:// URL that's a directory, then look for archives in the directory listing.
Links to VCS project URLs are not supported.

option.name: --group
tip: U: --group <[path:]group>
Install a named dependency-group from a 'pyproject.toml' file.
If a path is given, the name of the file must be 'pyproject.toml'.
Defaults to using 'pyproject.toml' in the current directory.

option.name: --ignore-requires-python
tip: Ignore the Requires-Python information.

option.name: --index-url
tip: U: -i, --index-url <url>
Base URL of the Python Package Index (default https://pypi.org/simple).
This should point to a repository compliant with PEP 503 (the simple repository API) or a local directory laid out in the same format.

option.name: --no-binary
tip: U: --no-binary <format_control>
Do not use binary packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all binary packages, ':none:' to empty the set (notice the colons), or one or more package names with commas between them (no colons).
Note that some packages are tricky to compile and may fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --no-build-isolation
tip: Disable isolation when building a modern source distribution.
Build dependencies specified by PEP 518 must be already installed if this option is used.

option.name: --no-clean
tip: Don't clean up build directories.

option.name: --no-deps
tip: Don't install package dependencies.

option.name: --no-index
tip: Ignore package index (only looking at --find-links URLs instead).

option.name: --no-verify
tip: Don't verify if built wheel is valid.

option.name: --only-binary
tip: U: --only-binary <format_control>
Do not use source packages.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable all source packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Packages without binary distributions will fail to install when this option is used on them.

option.name: --only-final
tip: U: --only-final <release_control>
Only allow final releases (no pre-releases) for a package.
Can be supplied multiple times, and each time adds to the existing value.
Accepts either ':all:' to disable pre-releases for all packages, ':none:' to empty the set, or one or more package names with commas between them.
Cannot be used with --pre.

option.name: --pre
tip: Include pre-release and development versions.
By default, pip only finds stable versions.

option.name: --prefer-binary
tip: Prefer binary packages over source packages, even if the source packages are newer.

option.name: --progress-bar
tip: U: --progress-bar <progress_bar>
Specify whether the progress bar should be used.
In 'auto' mode, --quiet will suppress all progress bars.

next.name: auto
tip: Default value

option.name: --require-hashes
tip: Require a hash to check each requirement against, for repeatable installs.
This option is implied when any package in a requirements file has a --hash option.

option.name: --requirement
tip: U: -r, --requirement <file>
Install from the given requirements file.
The file or URL can be in pip's requirements.txt format, or pylock.toml format.
This option can be used multiple times.

option.name: --requirements-from-script
tip: U: --requirements-from-script <file>
Install dependencies of the given script file as defined by PEP 723 inline metadata.

option.name: --src
tip: U: --src <dir>
Directory to check out editable projects into.
The default in a virtualenv is '<venv path>/src'.
The default for global installs is '<current dir>/src'.

option.name: --uploaded-prior-to
tip: U: --uploaded-prior-to <datetime_or_duration>
Only consider packages uploaded prior to the given value.
Accepts an ISO 8601 datetime (e.g., '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z', uses local timezone if none specified) or a duration in days (e.g., 'P3D' for packages uploaded at least 3 days ago).
Only effective when installing from indexes that provide upload-time metadata.

option.name: --wheel-dir
tip: U: -w, --wheel-dir <dir>
Build wheels into <dir>, where the default is the current working directory.

option.name: --version
tip: Show version and exit.

common_option.name: --cache-dir
tip: U: --cache-dir <dir>
Store the cache data in <dir>.

common_option.name: --cert
tip: U: --cert <path>
Path to PEM-encoded CA certificate bundle.
If provided, overrides the default.
See 'SSL Certificate Verification' in pip documentation for more information.

common_option.name: --client-cert
tip: U: --client-cert <path>
Path to SSL client certificate, a single file containing the private key and the certificate in PEM format.

common_option.name: --debug
tip: Let unhandled exceptions propagate outside the main subroutine, instead of logging them to stderr.

common_option.name: --disable-pip-version-check
tip: Don't periodically check PyPI to determine whether a new version of pip is available for download.
Implied with --no-index.

common_option.name: --exists-action
tip: U: --exists-action <action>
Default action when a path already exists:
(s)witch, (i)gnore, (w)ipe, (b)ackup, (a)bort.

common_option.name: --help
tip: Show help.

common_option.name: --isolated
tip: Run pip in an isolated mode, ignoring environment variables and user configuration.

common_option.name: --keyring-provider
tip: U: --keyring-provider <keyring_provider>
Enable the credential lookup via the keyring library if user input is allowed.
Specify which mechanism to use.

next.name: auto
tip: Default value

common_option.name: --log
tip: U: --log <path>
Path to a verbose appending log.

common_option.name: --no-cache-dir
tip: Disable the cache.

common_option.name: --no-color
tip: Suppress colored output.

common_option.name: --no-input
tip: Disable prompting for input.

common_option.name: --proxy
tip: U: --proxy <proxy>
Specify a proxy in the form scheme://[user:passwd@]proxy.server:port.

common_option.name: --python
tip: U: --python <python>
Run pip with the specified Python interpreter.

common_option.name: --quiet
tip: U: -q, --quiet
Give less output. Option is additive, and can be used up to 3 times.
(corresponding to WARNING, ERROR, and CRITICAL logging levels).

common_option.name: --require-virtualenv
tip: Allow pip to only run in a virtual environment; exit with an error otherwise.

common_option.name: --resume-retries
tip: U: --resume-retries <resume_retries>
Maximum attempts to resume or restart an incomplete download. (default: 5)

common_option.name: --retries
tip: U: --retries <retries>
Maximum attempts to establish a new HTTP connection. (default: 5)

common_option.name: --timeout
tip: U: --timeout <sec>
Set the socket timeout (default 15 seconds).

common_option.name: --trusted-host
tip: U: --trusted-host <hostname>
Mark this host or host:port pair as trusted, even though it does not have valid or any HTTPS.

common_option.name: --use-deprecated
tip: U: --use-deprecated <feature>
Enable deprecated functionality, that will be removed in the future.

common_option.name: --use-feature
tip: U: --use-feature <feature>
Enable new functionality, that may be backward incompatible.

common_option.name: --verbose
tip: U: -v, --verbose
Give more output. Option is additive, and can be used up to 3 times.

common_option.name: --version
tip: Show version and exit.
